issues.comment: Regex in `email_in_restriction_setting?` lacks `$` anchor, allowing domain suffix bypass. The regex `@(#{domains})` matches the domain as a substring rather than the full domain. For whitelists, an attacker can register with e.g. `attacker@company.com.evil.org` to bypass a `company.com` whitelist. For blacklists, legitimate emails like `user@spam.com.legitimate.org` are incorrectly blocked. Fix: anchor with `$` e.g. `regexp = Regexp.new("@(.+\\.)?(#{domains})$", true)`.
